content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : William R. Attwater                                    Date : December 20, 197
     Chief Counsel
     State Water Resources Control Board

                                                            A-77-513

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Kenneth W. Goshorn, Research
        Specialist III, Conflicts of Interest Division [signature]

Subject: Non-Substantive Alterations of the Conflict of Interest Code
          of the State Water Resources Control Board


        This is in response to your memorandum to the Executive
Director of the Commission dated November 28, 1977.

        Pursuant to 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18752, the alterations
in the Code referred to in your memorandum are deemed non-
substantive and may be made on an interim basis extending
through the calendar year 1978. This approval is based on
the fact that all the proposed changes appear to fall within
the confines of §18752(d). Such a conclusion involves inter-
pretation of subsections (d)(1) and (4) of the Regulation.
They provide:

        (d) Non-substantive alterations of conflict of
interest codes shall be limited to the following:

        (1) The reclassification or renaming of
previously designated positions, provided no
designated positions are created or deleted,
and provided no existing disclosure responsi-
bilities are modified; ...

        (4) The creation of new positions which
must be designated pursuant to Government Code
§8702(a), which are subject to an existing
agency disclosure category or categories; or
the transfer of a position previously desig-
nated to a more inclusive category; provided,
however, that any person occupying a position
designated pursuant to this subsection may
within 10 working days of notification of
designation file objection with the filing
officer of the agency. The filing of an
objection shall render the proposed desig-
nation ineffective until the provisions of
Government Code §8731l are satisfied with
respect to the designation of the position.

William R. Attwater
December 20, 1977                                     Page Two


     At first blush a reading of these sections would raise
a question whether positions are being "created or deleted"
within the meaning of the proviso in subsection (d)(1) and
hence the alteration could not be deemed non-substantive.
However, in light of the provisions of subsection (d)(4),
it is clear that the creation of certain types of positions
may be deemed non-substantive. Therefore, the Regulation
should be interpreted in the following fashion. The proviso
in subsection (d)(1) only applies to situations where a
position previously designated is to be removed from the
list of designated positions but continues in existence.
If the position is abolished totally, a change in the Code
to reflect that action shall be deemed non-substantive.
In terms of the creation of new positions, where the new
position or positions are subject to an existing disclosure
category subsection (d)(4) shall control. Subsection (d)(1)'s
proviso shall apply to the creation of positions with new
and different disclosure responsibilities. This clarification
having been made, it is apparent that the proposed alterations
fit within the meaning of §18752(d), or are mere renumbering
or corrections of typographical errors to reflect the sub-
stance of the Code as previously approved by the Commission.

     In light of the effective date of the Code these interim
non-substantive alterations shall remain effective until formal
hearing of the matter in early 1979.

     If I can be of further assistance in this or any other
matter please feel free to call on me at 322-6444.



KWG:glb

State of California

Memorandum

THE RESOURCES AGENCY

To : Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Date : NOV 23 1977

From : STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD

Subject: Request for Classification of Conflict of Interest Code Amendments
as Non-substantive

The State Water Resources Control Board is making final preparations to file and implement its Conflict of Interest Code. In the interim since the Code was approved by the FPPC (June 8, 1977) there has been some reorganization of the Board's staff. We would like to reflect this reorganization in our Code when it is filed with the Office of Administrative Hearings. (We hope to submit it on January 3, 1978.)

Therefore, we would like your approval of the amendments set forth below as non-substantive amendments pursuant to Section 18752(c) of your regulations. In accordance with Section 18752(b) of your regulations, these amendments will be submitted for permanent approval by the Commission itself by January 31 of 1979.

The amendments are as follows (page references are to pages of the Code as approved by the FPPC on June 8):

Executive Director                                    -3-

All future proposed non-substantive changes to our Code will be
submitted in strike out/underline form.  However, because Appendix
A of our Code as approved by the FPTC (our instruction manual for
our employees) is currently being placed on tape and is not yet
available, we ask that in the interest of time you take action on
these proposed changes in the form set forth above.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.


[illegible signature]
William R. Attwater
Chief Counsel

CC:  Michael S. Sloss
     Pete A. Rogers

sections.conclusion: Pursuant to 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18752, the alterations in the Code referred to in your memorandum are deemed non-substantive and may be approved on an interim basis extending through the calendar year 1978.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether proposed amendments to the State Water Resources Control Board's Conflict of Interest Code, necessitated by internal staff reorganization, qualify as non-substantive alterations under California Administrative Code Section 18752.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approves the proposed amendments as non-substantive alterations under §18752(d), interpreting the regulation to allow reclassification of positions, abolishment of positions, and creation of new positions subject to existing disclosure categories. The interim approval extends through 1978, with formal hearing scheduled for early 1979.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the FPPC response (dated December 20, 1977) and the incoming request memorandum (dated November 28, 1977). Extracted information focuses on the FPPC response. Original document has scanning artifacts and some text degradation, but key sections are legible. No explicit verbatim 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but content clearly functions as question and answer.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether proposed amendments to the State Water Resources Control Board's Conflict of Interest Code, necessitated by internal staff reorganization, qualify as non-substantive alterations under California Administrative Code Section 18752.

CONCLUSION: Pursuant to 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18752, the alterations in the Code referred to in your memorandum are deemed non-substantive and may be approved on an interim basis extending through the calendar year 1978.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to State Water Resources Control Board's request to classify Conflict of Interest Code amendments as non-substantive changes resulting from staff reorganization. The letter approves the interim non-substantive amendments and clarifies the interpretation of §18752(d) regarding position creation, deletion, and reclassification.
